title: Combination Sum II
description: Given a collection of candidate numbers (candidates) and a target number (target), find all unique combinations in candidates where the candidate numbers sum to target. Each number in candidates may only be used once in the combination. The solution set must not contain duplicate combinations.

examples.explanation: The unique combinations that sum up to 8 are: [1,1,6], [1,2,5], [1,7], and [2,6]. Note that [1,1,6] and [1,2,5] cannot be formed with repeated elements.
The valid combinations summing to 5 are [1,2,2] and [5]. The combination [2,2,1] is avoided because it is a duplicate of [1,2,2].
Only one candidate matches the target exactly, which is [1]. Thus, this is the only unique combination.

constraints: The size of candidates will be at most 100.
The value of target is a positive integer.
hints: Consider using backtracking to explore all potential combinations.
Sort the candidates array to facilitate skipping duplicates.
Use a set or a boolean array to track which elements have been used in the current combination.
Think about the base case for your recursive function to stop when you've hit the target.
